1	/* Drain markers and fail-fast helper shared by all C-level round-trip
2	 * fixtures (rt_c_hello.c, rt_c_printf.c, rt_c_file_io.c).
3	 *
4	 * The fixtures are timing-aware: only the span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain()
5	 * and rt_phase_vsim_drain() may touch libgloss / sim_control MMIO. Outside
6	 * that window the program runs on QEMU virt, which has no peripheral at
7	 * 0xE0080000.
8	 */
9	
10	#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
11	#define RT_C_HELPERS_H
12	
13	#include <stdint.h>
14	#include <stdio.h>
15	
16	/* Matches src/platform/sim_control.hpp:32 SimCommand::FAILED. */
17	#define SIM_CONTROL_REG (*(volatile uint32_t*) 0xE0080000)
18	#define SIM_FAILED 0x01234569u
19	
20	/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
21	 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
22	 *
23	 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
24	 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
25	 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
26	 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
27	static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
28	{
29	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
30	                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
31	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
32	}
33	static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
34	{
35	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
36	                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
37	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
38	}
39	
40	/* Vsim-only failure path: writes SimCommand::FAILED so vsim's
41	 * handle_sim_command (sim_control.hpp:188) sets exits=1 and the simulator
42	 * loop returns 1 before the handoff fires. The harness sees RC=1 vs
43	 * EXPECT_RC=0 and reports a failure. Bypasses QEMU2 entirely. */
44	__attribute__((noreturn)) static inline void rt_fail(void)
45	{
46	  fflush(stdout);
47	  SIM_CONTROL_REG = SIM_FAILED;
48	  for (;;) {
49	  }
50	}
51	
52	/* RISC-V semihosting calls. Only safe in the QEMU2 phase (after
53	 * rt_phase_vsim_drain): QEMU is spawned with -semihosting-config
54	 * enable=on,target=native (qemu_handback.hpp:131-132) so the magic
55	 * slli/ebreak/srai triple is intercepted before reaching mtvec. vsim
56	 * does NOT implement semihosting, so executing these inside the vsim
57	 * span would route to m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loop. */
58	static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
59	{
60	  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
61	  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
62	  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
63	                       ".option norvc\n"
64	                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
65	                       "ebreak\n"
66	                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
67	                       ".option pop\n"
68	                       :
69	                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
70	                       : "memory");
71	}
72	
73	#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */
74

1	/* Tier-1 C-level round-trip smoke test.
2	 *
3	 * Phase A (QEMU1): bring-up via crt0.S, hits csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -- plugin drains
4	 *   GPR/PC/CSR state and exits. No libgloss reachable.
5	 * Phase B (vsim):  printf via newlib stdio -> _write fd 1 -> per-byte poke to
6	 *   sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR (0xE0080004); hits csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -- vsim drains
7	 *   and spawns QEMU2.
8	 * Phase C (QEMU2): main() returns 0 -> crt0 calls _exit(0) -> dual-path _exit
9	 *   silently writes 0xE0080000 (no-op on QEMU virt) then issues semihosting
10	 *   SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED, terminating QEMU2 with status 0.
11	 */
12	
13	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
14	
15	int main(void)
16	{
17	  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();
18	
19	  /* Unbuffered so each byte hits sim_control before the vsim drain marker;
20	   * stdout is otherwise block-buffered when not connected to a tty. */
21	  setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
22	
23	  printf("hello from vsim\n");
24	
25	  rt_phase_vsim_drain();
26	  return 0;
27	}

1	/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
2	 *
3	 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
4	 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
5	 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
6	 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
7	 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
8	 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
9	 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
10	 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
11	 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
12	 *
13	 * V-state transport
14	 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
15	 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
16	 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
17	 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
18	 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
19	 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
20	 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
21	 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
22	 *   loading from the SEED region.
23	 *
24	 * Memory layout:
25	 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
26	 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
27	 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
28	 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
29	 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
30	 *
31	 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
32	 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
33	 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
34	 */
35	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
36	
37	#include <stdint.h>
38	
39	#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
40	#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
41	#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */
42	
43	static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
44	static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;
45	
46	#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL
47	
48	static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
49	{
50	  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
51	    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
52	}
53	
54	int main(void)
55	{
56	  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
57	   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
58	   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
59	   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
60	   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
61	   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
62	   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
63	   * trigger. */
64	  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
65	
66	  asm volatile(
67	    "li     a4, 16\n"
68	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
69	    "mv     t0, %0\n"
70	    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
71	    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
72	    :: "r"(SEED)
73	    : "memory", "t0", "a4");
74	
75	  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();
76	
77	  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
78	   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
79	   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
80	  asm volatile(
81	    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
82	    "li     a4, 16\n"
83	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
84	    "mv     t0, %0\n"
85	    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
86	    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
87	    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
88	    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
89	    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
90	    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
91	    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
92	    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
93	    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
94	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
95	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
96	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
97	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
98	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
99	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
100	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
101	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
102	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
103	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
104	    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
105	     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */

107	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
108	    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"

/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

1	/* rt_c_v_csrs.c - Round-trip fixture: V scalar CSR survival.
2	 *
3	 * Exercises vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vtype, vl, and vstart across the
4	 * QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 handoff.
5	 *
6	 * Phase 1 (QEMU1): drain marker only (no V instructions; plugin PC
7	 *   drain constraint).
8	 * Phase 2 (vsim):  configure known V CSR values, then drain.
9	 *   - vsetvli: vl=8, vtype=e32/m1/ta/ma (0xD0)
10	 *   - csrw vxsat=1, vxrm=2, vstart=3 (vstart last: vsetvli resets it to 0)
11	 * Phase 3 (QEMU2): read V CSRs via csrr and verify each expected value.
12	 *   mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no extra csrs needed.
13	 *   vstart is read before any V instruction so it holds the restored value.
14	 *
15	 * vcsr[0] = vxsat, vcsr[2:1] = vxrm -> expected vcsr = (2<<1)|1 = 5.
16	 * vtype: vsew=e32=010 at bits[5:3], vta=1 at bit6, vma=1 at bit7 -> 0xD0.
17	 */
18	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
19	
20	#include <stdint.h>
21	
22	int main(void)
23	{
24	  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): no V instructions before the drain marker. */
25	  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();
26	
27	  /* Phase 2 (vsim): set known V CSR values.
28	   * crt0.S already set mstatus.VS=non-Off so vsetvli doesn't trap.
29	   * vstart must be written last: vsetvli resets vstart to 0. */
30	  asm volatile(
31	    "li     a0, 8\n"
32	    "vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"  /* vl=8, vtype=0xD0 */
33	    "li     t1, 1\n"
34	    "csrw   vxsat, t1\n"
35	    "li     t1, 2\n"
36	    "csrw   vxrm,  t1\n"
37	    "li     t1, 3\n"
38	    "csrw   vstart, t1\n"
39	    ::: "t0", "t1", "a0", "memory");
40	
41	  rt_phase_vsim_drain();
42	
43	  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): verify V CSRs survived the round-trip.
44	   * Read vstart first, before any V instruction resets it to 0. */
45	  uint64_t vstart_out, vxsat_out, vxrm_out, vcsr_out, vtype_out, vl_out;
46	  asm volatile(
47	    "csrr %0, vstart\n"
48	    "csrr %1, vxsat\n"
49	    "csrr %2, vxrm\n"
50	    "csrr %3, vcsr\n"
51	    "csrr %4, vtype\n"
52	    "csrr %5, vl\n"
53	    : "=r"(vstart_out), "=r"(vxsat_out), "=r"(vxrm_out),
54	      "=r"(vcsr_out),   "=r"(vtype_out), "=r"(vl_out));
55	
56	  int ok = 1;
57	  if (vl_out     != 8u)    { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vl\n");     ok = 0; }
58	  if (vxsat_out  != 1u)    { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vxsat\n");  ok = 0; }
59	  if (vxrm_out   != 2u)    { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vxrm\n");   ok = 0; }
60	  if (vcsr_out   != 5u)    { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vcsr\n");   ok = 0; }
61	  if (vtype_out  != 0xD0u) { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vtype\n");  ok = 0; }
62	  if (vstart_out != 3u)    { rt_semihost_writes("FAIL vstart\n"); ok = 0; }
63	
64	  if (ok)
65	    rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_csrs: PASS\n");
66	  else
67	    rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_csrs: FAIL\n");
68	  return ok ? 0 : 1;
69	}

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip:
3	#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
4	#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#
10	# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
11	#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
12	#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
13	#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
14	#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
15	#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
16	#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
17	#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
18	#
19	# Fixture-parameterized:
20	#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
21	#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
22	#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
23	#
24	# Required environment:
25	#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
26	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
27	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
28	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
29	#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
34	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
35	
36	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
37	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
38	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
39	
40	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
41	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
42	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
43	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
44	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
45	
46	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
47	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
48	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
49	    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
50	  fi
51	done
52	
53	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
54	  csr) ;;
55	  pc)
56	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
57	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
58	      exit 77
59	    fi
60	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
61	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
62	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
63	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
64	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
65	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
66	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
67	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
68	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
69	      exit 1
70	    fi
71	    ;;
72	  *)
73	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
74	    exit 1
75	    ;;
76	esac
77	
78	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
79	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
80	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
81	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
82	
83	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
84	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
85	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
86	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
87	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)

  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

1	# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
2	# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...
3	
4	TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
5	CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
6	LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
7	OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump
8	
9	CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
10	LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static
11	
12	# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
13	RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
14	               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
15	               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival
16	
17	# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
18	# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
19	RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
20	RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
21	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
22	# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
23	PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
24	RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
25	RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
26	                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
27	RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
28	                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
29	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
30	# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
31	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
32	                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
33	
34	.PHONY: all clean dump
35	
36	all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
37	     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
38	     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
39	     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)
40	
41	spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
42		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
43		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@
44	
45	handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
46		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
47		$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@
48	
49	handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
50		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
51		$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@
52	
53	handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
54		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
55		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@
56	
57	$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
58		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
59		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
60	
61	# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
62	# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
63	# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
64	# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
65	# the .text wildcard.
66	$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
67		$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@
68	
69	# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
70	# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
71	$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
72		$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@
73	
74	# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
75	PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
76	$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
77		$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
78		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
79	
80	dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

1	// Shared exit-via-semihosting macro for hybrid round-trip fixtures.
2	//
3	// SEMIHOST_EXIT exit_reg
4	//   Builds the {reason=ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit, exit_code=<exit_reg>}
5	//   16-byte block at 0x80001000, sets a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (0x20), a1=block,
6	//   then issues the RISC-V semihosting `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`
7	//   triple. exit_reg may be any GPR holding the desired host exit code; this
8	//   macro clobbers t0, t1, a0, a1.
9	//
10	// QEMU's host-exit returns the low 8 bits of <exit_reg> as the process status.
11	
12	.macro SEMIHOST_EXIT exit_reg
13	    li      t0, 0x80001000
14	    li      t1, 0x20026                     // ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit
15	    sd      t1, 0(t0)
16	    sd      \exit_reg, 8(t0)
17	    li      a0, 0x20                        // SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED
18	    mv      a1, t0
19	    .option push
20	    .option norvc
21	    slli    x0, x0, 0x1f
22	    ebreak
23	    srai    x0, x0, 0x7
24	    .option pop
25	99: j       99b
26	.endm

2	//
3	// Tier-1 fixture: M-mode CSRs survive QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2.
4	// Verifies the CSR pass-through merge in HandoffController and the
5	// feature-aware P-packet path in qemu_handback.
6	//
7	// Phase A (QEMU1): write distinguishable values to mscratch, mtvec, and
8	//                  mepc, then trigger the entry handoff.
9	// Phase B (vsim):  no-op.  vsim does not modify these CSRs; they must
10	//                  pass through the input-state snapshot kept by
11	//                  HandoffController and arrive at QEMU 2 unchanged.
12	// Phase C (QEMU2): csrr each CSR back into a GPR, compare. Exit code is
13	//                  the CSR index of the first mismatch (1=mscratch,
14	//                  2=mtvec, 3=mepc), 0 on full pass.
15	//
16	// mtvec is written with a 4-byte aligned value (mode=Direct) so QEMU
17	// accepts it without rounding the low bits.
18	
19	#include "semihost_exit.inc"
20	
21	.section .text
22	.globl _start
23	_start:
24	    li      t0, 0xCAFEBABEDEADBEEF
25	    csrw    mscratch, t0             // mscratch <- 0xCAFE...
26	
27	    li      t0, 0x80000004           // 4-byte aligned, mode=Direct
28	    csrw    mtvec,    t0
29

31	    csrw    mepc,     t0
32

44	    bne     t1, t2, fail_mscratch

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do

    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"

  pc)

    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')

      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1

    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1

STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)

  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")

#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.

#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1

  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#

# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#

#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"

    exit 77
  fi

  csr) ;;

/* Drain markers and helpers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule (enforced by inspection):
 *   The span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain() and rt_phase_vsim_drain()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT perform any I/O -- no libgloss / sim_control
 *   MMIO (0xE0080000+), no RISC-V semihosting. vsim does not implement
 *   semihosting, and emitting status bytes during the cycle-accurate span
 *   pollutes the round-trip with non-architectural events.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before rt_phase_qemu1_drain) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after rt_phase_vsim_drain) via rt_semihost_writes(). Both QEMU
 *   launches are configured with -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native
 *   (roundtrip_e2e.sh, rt_shared_mem.sh, qemu_handback.hpp).
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

#include <stdint.h>

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

/* RISC-V semihosting write-string. Safe in QEMU1 (phase 1) and QEMU2
 * (phase 3); both QEMUs are spawned with -semihosting-config
 * enable=on,target=native so the magic slli/ebreak/srai triple is
 * intercepted before reaching mtvec. MUST NOT be called inside the
 * vsim span (between rt_phase_qemu1_drain and rt_phase_vsim_drain):
 * vsim does not implement semihosting, the ebreak routes to
 * m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loops. */
static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
{
  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
                       ".option norvc\n"
                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
                       "ebreak\n"
                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
                       ".option pop\n"
                       :
                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
                       : "memory");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)

  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
                       ".option norvc\n"
                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
                       "ebreak\n"
                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
                       ".option pop\n"
                       :
                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
                       : "memory");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)

      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.

    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_file_io|FIXTURE=rt_c_file_io EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
